name: USP30 inhibition
summary: Small-molecule inhibition of the mitochondrial deubiquitinase USP30, intended to lower the threshold for PINK1/Parkin-linked mitochondrial ubiquitination and mitophagy.
mechanism_summary: USP30 opposes mitochondrial ubiquitination; inhibiting it can increase mitophagy markers in cells and reporter mice, but current evidence is preclinical and a 2026 cybrid study did not significantly reduce mutant mtDNA load.

open_questions: Whether USP30 inhibition can be dosed safely enough for chronic aging-relevant use
Whether mitophagy induction produces functional benefit rather than only mitolysosome or ubiquitination-marker movement
Whether this mechanism can reduce pathogenic or age-associated mitochondrial damage burdens in vivo
